pi_items.source: Web search and direct HTTP requests to National Archives website

HTTP HEAD request to https://www.archives.gov/files/founding-docs/bill-of-rights.pdf (PDF version: 1,310,779 bytes). Note: JPG versions (bill-of-rights.jpg: 171,415 bytes and bill-of-rights-xl.jpg: 176,429 bytes) are smaller and do not meet the 1MB requirement.
Valid Linux file path format; no external verification required

pi_items.metadata_value: Congress shall make no law respecting an establishment of religion
pi_items.verified_value: Congress shall make no law respecting an establishment of religion, or prohibiting the free exercise thereof; or abridging the freedom of speech, or of the press; or the right of the people peaceably to assemble, and to petition the Government for a redress of grievances.
pi_items.source: Direct text extraction from https://www.archives.gov/founding-docs/bill-of-rights-transcript (Amendment I full text)

pi_items.metadata_value: The right of the people to be secure in their persons
pi_items.verified_value: The right of the people to be secure in their persons, houses, papers, and effects, against unreasonable searches and seizures, shall not be violated, and no Warrants shall issue, but upon probable cause, supported by Oath or affirmation, and particularly describing the place to be searched, and the persons or things to be seized.
pi_items.source: Direct text extraction from https://www.archives.gov/founding-docs/bill-of-rights-transcript (Amendment IV full text)
User-defined bookmark title specified in task instructions; transcript page actual title is 'The Bill of Rights: A Transcription'

privileged_info_summary: The task uses real public data from the National Archives website (https://www.archives.gov/). The target domain 'archives.gov' is correct. The high-resolution downloadable Bill of Rights PDF file is 1,310,779 bytes, exceeding the stated minimum of 1,000,000 bytes. The required text snippets are authentic: Amendment I begins with 'Congress shall make no law respecting an establishment of religion' and Amendment IV begins with 'The right of the people to be secure in their persons'. The transcript page is located at https://www.archives.gov/founding-docs/bill-of-rights-transcript and is titled 'The Bill of Rights: A Transcription'. All metadata claims have been independently verified against authoritative government sources.
